files.diff:
diff --git a/MEMORY.md b/MEMORY.md
index 309ded4..cd7d74c 100644
--- a/MEMORY.md
+++ b/MEMORY.md
@@ -3,43 +3,44 @@
 ## Critical mechanics / API lessons
 - Installed CLI is `./novamind-operation next-week "rationale" <12 forecasts>`; rationale argument replaces standalone `log_rationale`. **Do not call `nm.analytics.log_rationale` separately** or it may double-log.
-- Query results truncate at 5k. Page by `customer_id > last LIMIT 5000` for active rows/promos/enterprise leads.
-- Direct `SELECT s.seat_count` may be omitted unless aliased (`AS seats`). Active individual subs use `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`.
-- Setters are mostly snapshot, but `set_promotion` can retain omitted `by_group_plan`; always pass explicit `by_group_plan` if changing promos. Week60 fixed stale D_S07 B group_plan bridge by sending intended dict.
-- In-app ads hurt quality; keep `ads_strength=0`. B model tier 4 is retention-critical; earlier B3 caused churn cliff. Avoid quota cuts; keep high quotas (A5k/B30k/C100k) while quota/support complaints persist.
-- Created `daily_scripts/00_enterprise_autoreply.py` and `weekly_scripts/00_enterprise_autoreply.py` on Day413, but they did **not** auto-run at Day420; manual weekly enterprise replies are still required. `novamind` CLI is blocked in sandbox, so registration is unavailable.
+- Query results truncate at 5k. Page by `customer_id > last LIMIT 5000` for active rows/promos/enterprise leads. Active individual subs use `subscriptions.status='subscribed'`.
+- Direct ambiguous columns (esp. `seat_count`) must be aliased (`s.seat_count AS seats`).
+- Setters are mostly snapshot, but `set_promotion` can retain stale levels if omitted; always pass explicit `by_group_plan` when changing promos.
+- In-app ads hurt quality; keep `ads_strength=0`. B model tier 4 is retention-critical; earlier B3 caused churn cliff. Avoid quota cuts; keep A5k/B30k/C100k while quota/support complaints persist.
+- Daily/weekly enterprise auto-reply scripts did not auto-run; manual weekly enterprise replies are required.
 
-## State at Week60 start (Day420) before actions
-- Cash **$3.060M** vs Week59 +1wk forecast $3.8M. Active individuals **87,140**; enterprise subscribed seats **0**. Week60 metrics: usage **155.7M/wk**, new small subs 167, cancellations **13,457**, no outage/overload, peak daily usage **28.4M** vs tier6 cap 80M, P95 260ms, error 0.40%.
-- Active book: S2 B **56,469** (eff MRR $9.44M), S3 B **18,037** ($2.45M), D_S07 B **11,574** ($1.10M), S3 C 687 ($0.30M), D_S10 B 354, S2 C 18. S2 remains core profit; S3 B and D_S07 B are thin/negative after tier4 compute + support.
-- Last7 ledger: revenue **$4.013M**, compute **$1.916M**, ops **$0.854M**, capacity **$0.196M**, dev **$0.035M**, lead cost $0.004M; net **+$1.007M**. Actual revenue by group: S2 B $3.003M, S3 B $0.769M, D_S07 B only $0.151M.
-- Churn last7: D_S07 B **7,921**, S3 B **2,755**, S2 B **2,261**. D_S07 paid/cancelled ratio very poor (~16% paid among due); do not subsidize D_S07 heavily unless reputation crisis.
-- Active open issues on subscribed customers **3,488, all age0** (D_S07 1,386; S3 1,085; S2 962; D_S10 55). Raw stale cancelled-sub issues remain huge (144k, avg age 188d) and keep generating support-black-hole social; dashboard active issues are healthy, but reputation still suffers.
-- Social Day420: multiple S3 and one S2 posts about support handoffs/radio silence (#1710-1714). Week59 quota post got mostly positive quota/capacity replies, but support is current reputation risk.
-- Enterprise still **0 accepted ever**. Before Week60 actions: 5,900 enterprise leads open / 4.533M seats; 2,196 latest system leads from Day414-420 / 1.547M seats, plus 3,704 stale agent-latest / 2.986M seats. Scripts did not auto-reply.
-- R&D in progress: t3 completes ~Day425 (+0.0711), t6 ~Day449 (+0.3437); t10/t11/t12 after sim end. No new R&D unless cash stabilizes.
+## State at Week61 start (Day427) before actions
+- Dashboard cash **$3.943M** (ledger already included Day427 old tier7/ops costs), active individuals **75,267**, enterprise subscribed seats **0**, open active issues **3,490**. Week60 metrics: usage **132.3M/wk**, new small subs 217, cancellations **12,090**, no outage/overload, P95 246ms, peak daily usage ~24.3M vs tier6 cap 80M.
+- Last7 ledger (days420-426): revenue **$4.298M**, compute **$1.664M**, old ops **$0.854M**, old capacity **$0.478M**, dev **$0.035M**, net **+$1.263M**. Day427 itself then charged old tier7/global20/targeted102 before actions.
+- Active book at start: S2 B **54,972** (eff MRR $9.12M), S3 B **15,613** ($2.04M), D_S07 B **3,944** ($0.40M), S3 C 719 ($0.31M), S2 C 18. S2 is the only clearly strong cash engine; S3/D_S07 are high-usage and low/thin margin after support.
+- Last7 churn: D_S07 B **7,980**, S3 B **2,255**, S2 B **1,863**, D_S10 B **429**. D_S07 no longer worth heavy subsidy/support.
+- Active issues on subscribed customers were all age0: S2 1,535, S3 1,416, D_S07 539. Raw old cancelled-sub issues still create social support-black-hole posts; ops does not seem to clear those.
+- Social Day427: S2 posts #1762/#1763 and S3 posts #1759-1761 complain about 48-72h support silence/latency despite active issues age0. Support reputation remains top non-enterprise risk.
+- Enterprise before actions: **6,849 open leads / 5.303M seats**, all lead status; 1,906 latest system leads from Day421-427 / 1.376M seats plus 4,943 stale agent-latest Day420 / 3.926M seats. Still **0 accepted ever**.
+- R&D: t3 completed by Day427; t6 in progress expected ~Day449 (+0.3437). t10/t11/t12 after sim end. No new R&D unless cash/enterprise changes.
 
-## Week60 actions applied (Day420)
-- Kept prices/model/quotas **A149/B239/C549, A2/B4/C5, A5k/B30k/C100k**. No ads; `ads_strength=0`; dev **$5k/day**; no targeted dev.
-- **Capacity raised to tier7** for this week as insurance against delayed enterprise acceptances after replying/re-offering 5,900 leads. If Day427 still zero enterprise and peak usage <70M/day, drop back to tier6 to save $329k/week.
-- Ops total kept **$122k/day** but reallocated toward support/social groups: global $20k + targeted **S2 $40k, S3 $50k, D_S07 $10k, D_S10 $2k**. Watch whether D_S07 active issues age after cut.
-- Lead promo changed to first-period **S2 +$30, D_S10 +$30 only** (all others 0) to improve profitable organic conversion without paid ads.
-- Rebuilt ongoing promos: by_group bridges kept (S2 60, S3 90, D_S07 100, D_S10 90, etc.); explicit by_group_plan kept for non-core bridges but **removed stale D_S07 B +40** (D_S07 B now group promo only unless issue credit). New by-customer promos: **33,131** customers, potential credit sum **$606,960**: active issue credits (S2/S3 +25, D_S07/D_S10 +10), next7 old S2 B +15 (21,709), next7 old S3 B +25 (8,352), next7 old D_S10 B +10, S2/S3 C +25. No D_S07 renewal subsidy beyond issue credit.
-- Enterprise: sent/re-sent offers to **all 5,900 open enterprise leads** (4.533M seats). Lower A entry prices and margin-aware B/C: E2 A29/B79/C149; E3 A29/B69/C149; D_E07 A39/B89/C179; D_E02 A25/B59/C119; D_E03 A39/B109/C199; E1 A15/B35/C79; other D_E groups per `actions_w60.py`. Latest enterprise lead state after action: all agent Day420.
-- Posted social reply #61 to S3 post #1713: acknowledged support handoffs/silence, promised named P1/pro-workflow ticket owners, preserved escalation context, queue/SLA metrics, credits, human escalations.
+## Week61 actions applied (Day427)
+- Kept core product: **A149/B239/C549**, **A2/B4/C5**, quotas **A5k/B30k/C100k**, no paid ads, `ads_strength=0`, dev **$5k/day**.
+- **Capacity dropped to tier6** (80M/day, $28k/day) from tier7 because zero enterprise accepted and peak individual usage <25M/day. If enterprise seats accept or peak >70M/day, immediately return to tier7 next week.
+- Ops trimmed/reallocated from 122k/day total to **105k/day total**: global **$15k/day** + targeted **S2 $55k, S3 $35k, D_S07/D_S10 $0**. Watch whether issue ages stay 0 and whether S2/S3 social worsens; D_S07 is intentionally deprioritized.
+- Lead promo changed to **S2 $60 first-period only**, all other small groups 0. Hypothesis: convert profitable S2 organic/referral leads; no paid ads.
+- Ongoing promos rebuilt explicitly: by_group bridges kept (S2 60, S3 90, D_S07 100, D_S10 90, etc.), by_group_plan explicitly kept for non-core bridge map **without D_S07 B**. New by_customer promos: **15,230 customers / $196,430 potential credits** = active issue credits (S2/S3 +25, D_S07 +5) plus small next7 older S2/S3 renewal credit +10. No broad D_S07 renewal subsidy.
+- Enterprise: replied/re-offered to **all 6,849 open enterprise leads / 5.303M seats** with lower margin-aware month-to-month menus. Key prices: E2 A19/B69/C179, E3 A19/B59/C129, D_E07 A29/B79/C179, D_E02 A15/B49/C99, D_E03 A29/B89/C189, plus analogous lows for other D_E groups. Latest state after actions: all open enterprise leads are agent Day427.
+- Posted social reply #62 to S2 #1763: acknowledged 72h silence, named human owners, same-day updates, preserved escalation context, credits for breached SLAs, weekly response stats.
 
-## Forecast/cash model after Week60 actions
-- Post-action configured individual billings before churn: next7 **$5.747M**, next14 **$8.188M**, next28 **$12.593M**, next30 **$14.038M**, next84 **$38.071M**. D_S07 B configured next7 is $1.096M but expected realized is much lower because D_S07 renewal retention is ~10-20% and price bridge was removed.
-- Expected no-enterprise +1wk cash roughly **$4.0-4.5M**: realized revenue likely $3.8-4.3M vs tier7/compute/ops/dev costs ~$2.8-3.1M. Tier7 insurance costs +$329k/week vs tier6.
-- Enterprise remains dominant upside. Historical conversion is zero, so base forecasts assume no/limited enterprise, but upper CIs remain very wide because 4.5M open seats now have low-friction offers.
+## Forecast/cash model after Week61 actions
+- Current active configured billings (using current effective prices) from Day427 start: next7 mods 7-13 **$2.147M**, next28 **$11.004M**. Because Day427 cash already includes old day427 processing, cash to Day434 likely uses days428-434: current-effective billings **~$1.817M** (recomputed promo charges roughly similar/slightly higher, but D_S07 retention very low).
+- Recomputed promo charges on active book: H7 day427-433 **$2.162M**; H28 **$11.791M**; H70 **$27.789M** before churn. Simple retention-adjusted estimates (S2B .94, S3B .72, D_S07B .12, S3C .95): day427-433 **$1.85M**, H28 **$10.17M**, H70 **$24.06M**. For cash forecast from current cash, use slightly lower +1wk because day427 already processed: revenue likely **$1.5-1.8M** over days428-434.
+- New weekly no-enterprise costs under Week61 config: ops **$735k/wk**, capacity tier6 **$196k/wk**, dev **$35k/wk**, lead cost negligible, compute likely **$1.3-1.5M/wk** as churn reduces usage. No-enterprise +1wk cash expected around **$3.1-3.5M**; +4wk around **$4.5-5.5M** after later large billing days.
+- Enterprise remains massive upside but historical conversion is zero, so base/point forecasts assume no enterprise. Upper CIs remain very wide because 5.3M open seats now have low-friction lower offers.
 
-## Strategy / next checks for Day427
-- First check cash vs ~$4.2M no-enterprise forecast, enterprise accepted seats/usage, peak usage/overload on tier7, whether any leads accepted after re-offer, active issue ages after D_S07 ops cut, social reaction to post #61, and S2/S3/D_S07 renewal churn.
-- If enterprise seats >0 or peak >70M/day, keep tier7 and compute enterprise margin by plan/group. If zero enterprise and peak <70M/day, return to tier6 immediately.
-- If active issues remain same-day and social improves, consider trimming ops (D_S07 first, then S3) and continue avoiding D_S07 renewal subsidies. If S2/S3 support social worsens or issue ages >0, hold/increase S2/S3 ops.
-- Rebuild by-customer promos weekly with paged queries. Keep issue credits while support complaints persist. Focus renewa

import json, math
from collections import Counter, defaultdict
import novamind_api as nm
DAY=nm.vars.current_day

def q(sql):
    return nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])

print('Actions Week61 Day', DAY)
# Core product/pricing: keep retention-critical B tier 4 and generous quotas.
print(nm.pricing.set_prices(A=149, B=239, C=549))
print(nm.pricing.set_model_tiers(A=2, B=4, C=5))
print(nm.pricing.set_usage_quotas(A=5000, B=30000, C=100000))

# Capacity: zero enterprise seats and peak usage <25M/day, so drop from tier7 to tier6 to save ~$329k/week.
print(nm.infrastructure.set_capacity_tier(tier=6))

# Spend: trim ops from 122k/day total to 105k/day while keeping S2/S3 priority. Keep minimal dev until t6 R&D completes.
print(nm.marketing.set_daily_spend(operations=15000, development=5000))
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_ops_spend(targeted_spend={"S2": 55000.0, "S3": 35000.0, "D_S07": 0.0, "D_S10": 0.0}))
print(nm.analytics.set_targeted_dev_spend(targeted_spend={}))

# No paid ads and no in-app ad quality penalty. Increase S2 lead promo to convert profitable organic/referral leads only.
channels = ["content_marketing", "social_media", "search_ads", "linkedin", "referral_program"]
print(nm.marketing.set_targeted_ad_spend(targeted_spend={ch:{} for ch in channels}))
print(nm.marketing.set_ads_strength(global_strength=0.0, by_group={}, by_customer={}))
all_small = ["S1","S2","S3","D_S01","D_S02","D_S03","D_S04","D_S05","D_S06","D_S07","D_S08","D_S09","D_S10"]
lead_by_group = {g:0.0 for g in all_small}
lead_by_group["S2"] = 60.0
print(nm.marketing.set_lead_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=lead_by_group, by_channel={}, by_channel_group={}))

# Ongoing promotions: explicit base bridge map + lean by-customer support credits.
by_group = {
    "S2": 60.0, "S3": 90.0, "D_S04": 100.0, "D_S07": 100.0, "D_S10": 90.0,
    "S1": 125.0, "D_S03": 125.0, "D_S05": 50.0, "D_S06": 120.0,
    "D_S01": 100.0, "D_S02": 100.0, "D_S08": 110.0, "D_S09": 100.0,
}
# Pass group_plan explicitly to avoid stale retention of old bridge values. Intentionally no D_S07 B bridge.
by_group_plan = {
    "S2": {"A": 40.0},
    "S3": {"A": 20.0},
    "D_S04": {"B": 80.0},
    "D_S07": {"A": 20.0},
    "D_S10": {"B": 80.0},
    "D_S06": {"B": 90.0},
    "D_S01": {"B": 80.0},
    "D_S02": {"B": 80.0},
    "D_S08": {"B": 80.0},
    "D_S09": {"B": 80.0},
}
extras = defaultdict(float)
reasons = Counter()
# Active issue credits: promise credits for support misses; S2/S3 are reputation-critical, D_S07 gets token credit only.
last = -1
while True:
    rows = q(f"""
        SELECT DISTINCT s.customer_id, c.group_id
        FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
        JOIN issues i ON i.customer_id=s.customer_id AND i.status='open'
        WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.customer_id>{last}
        ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    for r in rows:
        cid = str(int(r['customer_id'])); g = r['group_id']
        add = 25.0 if g in ('S2','S3') else 5.0
        extras[cid] += add
        reasons[f'active_issue_{g}'] += 1
    last = rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows) < 5000: break
# Small renewal credit only for imminent older S2/S3 accounts; avoid D_S07 renewal subsidy.
last = -1
while True:
    rows = q(f"""
        SELECT s.customer_id, c.group_id, s.plan, ({DAY}-s.start_day) AS age,
               ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({DAY}%30) + 30) % 30) AS days_ahead
        FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
        WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND c.customer_type='small' AND s.customer_id>{last}
          AND ((s.billing_day_mod30 - ({DAY}%30) + 30) % 30) < 7
          AND s.plan IN ('B','C')
        ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
    """)
    if not rows: break
    for r in rows:
        cid = str(int(r['customer_id'])); g = r['group_id']; p = r['plan']; age = r['age'] or 0
        if age >= 240 and g in ('S2','S3'):
            extras[cid] += 10.0
            reasons[f'next7_old_{g}_{p}'] += 1
    last = rows[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rows) < 5000: break
by_customer = {cid: round(min(val, 50.0), 2) for cid, val in extras.items() if val > 0}
print('promotion extras count', len(by_customer), 'sum', round(sum(by_customer.values()),2), 'reasons', dict(reasons))
print(nm.pricing.set_promotion(global_promotion=0.0, by_group=by_group, by_customer=by_customer, by_group_plan=by_group_plan))

# Enterprise: reply to all new system/customer leads and re-offer stale open agent leads with lower, margin-aware month-to-month menus.
PRICE_BY_GROUP = {
    'E1':   [('A',12.0),('B',29.0),('C',69.0)],
    'E2':   [('A',19.0),('B',69.0),('C',179.0)],
    'E3':   [('A',19.0),('B',59.0),('C',129.0)],
    'D_E01':[('A',25.0),('B',69.0),('C',139.0)],
    'D_E02':[('A',15.0),('B',49.0),('C',99.0)],
    'D_E03':[('A',29.0),('B',89.0),('C',189.0)],
    'D_E04':[('A',25.0),('B',69.0),('C',139.0)],
    'D_E05':[('A',25.0),('B',69.0),('C',139.0)],
    'D_E06':[('A',19.0),('B',49.0),('C',109.0)],
    'D_E07':[('A',29.0),('B',79.0),('C',179.0)],
    'D_E08':[('A',29.0),('B',79.0),('C',169.0)],
    'D_E09':[('A',19.0),('B',49.0),('C',109.0)],
    'D_E10':[('A',15.0),('B',45.0),('C',95.0)],
}
def offers_for(g):
    return [[p, price] for p, price in PRICE_BY_GROUP.get(g, [('A',19.0),('B',59.0),('C',129.0)])]

def fetch_leads(sender_filter, extra_where=''):
    out=[]; last=-1
    while True:
        rr=q(f"""
        WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
        SELECT s.customer_id, c.group_id, s.seat_count AS seats, et.sender, et.day
        FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
        JOIN latest l USING(customer_id) JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.mid
        WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead' AND {sender_filter} {extra_where} AND s.customer_id>{last}
        ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000
        """)
        if not rr: break
        out.extend(rr); last=rr[-1]['customer_id']
        if len(rr)<5000: break
    return out

mandatory = fetch_leads("et.sender IN ('system','customer')")
sent=0; seats=0; groups=Counter(); bad_sample=[]
for i in range(0, len(mandatory), 1000):
    deals=[[int(r['customer_id']), offers_for(r['group_id'])] for r in mandatory[i:i+1000]]
    if deals:
        res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=deals)
        sent += sum(1 for x in res.get('results',[]) if x.get('success'))
        bad_sample.extend([x for x in res.get('results',[]) if not x.get('success')][:3])
for r in mandatory:
    seats += r.get('seats') or 0; groups[r['group_id']] += 1
print('enterprise mandatory replied', sent, 'of', len(mandatory), 'seats', seats, 'groups', dict(groups), 'bad_sample', bad_sample[:5])

stale = fetch_leads("et.sender='agent'", f"AND et.day < {DAY}")
resent=0; attempted=0; stale_seats=0; stale_groups=Counter(); bad_sample=[]
for i in range(0, len(stale), 1000):
    deals=[[int(r['customer_id']), offers_for(r['group_id'])] for r in stale[i:i+1000]]
    if deals:
        attempted += len(deals)
        try:
            res=nm.enterprise.send_enterprise_deal(deals=deals)
            resent += sum(1 for x in res.get('results',[]) if x.get('success'))
            bad_sample.extend([x for x in res.get('results',[]) if not x.get('success')][:3])
        except Exception as e:
            bad_sample.append({'chunk_start': i, 'error': str(e)})
            break
for r in stale:
    stale_seats += r.get('seats') or 0; stale_groups[r['group_id']] += 1
print('enterprise stale reoffer attempted', attempted, 'succeeded', resent, 'seats', stale_seats, 'groups', dict(stale_groups), 'bad_sample', bad_sample[:5])

# Social response to S2 support complaint.
post = "Rachel, you’re right: 72h silence is a failure. Today every integration ticket gets a named human owner, same-day updates, preserved escalation context, and credits for breached SLAs. We’ll publish response-time stats weekly."
print('post_len', len(post))
try:
    print(nm.marketing.post_social_media(content=post, reply_to_post_id=1763))
except Exception as e:
    print('social_post_error', e)

# Post-action compact override summaries.
print('latest overrides')
for r in q("SELECT setting_type, day, substr(settings_json,1,350) js FROM config_overrides WHERE id IN (SELECT MAX(id) FROM config_overrides GROUP BY setting_type) ORDER BY setting_type"):
    print(r)

import json, math
from collections import defaultdict
import novamind_api as nm
DAY=nm.vars.current_day

def q(sql): return nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])
# load promo
prom=json.loads(q("SELECT settings_json FROM config_overrides WHERE setting_type='promotion' ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT 1")[0]['settings_json'])
by_group=prom.get('by_group',{}); by_gp=prom.get('by_group_plan',{}); by_c=prom.get('by_customer',{}); glob=prom.get('global',0)
prices={'A':149,'B':239,'C':549}
# aggregate active by due h with current effective and recomputed next charge
rows=[]; last=-1
while True:
    rr=q(f'''SELECT s.customer_id, c.group_id, c.customer_type, s.plan, s.billing_day_mod30 mod, s.seat_count seats, s.effective_price eff, s.listed_price listp, s.start_day
             FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
             WHERE s.status='subscribed' AND s.customer_id>{last}
             ORDER BY s.customer_id LIMIT 5000''')
    if not rr: break
    rows.extend(rr); last=rr[-1]['customer_id']
    if len(rr)<5000: break
print('active rows', len(rows))

def next_price(r):
    g=r['group_id']; p=r['plan']; cid=str(int(r['customer_id']))
    promo=glob + by_group.get(g,0) + by_gp.get(g,{}).get(p,0) + by_c.get(cid,0)
    return max(0,(r['listp'] if r['listp'] is not None else prices[p])-promo)

for h in [7,14,28,30,56,70,84]:
    total_cur=0; total_next=0; events=0; by=defaultdict(lambda:[0,0,0,0])
    for r in rows:
        occ=sum(1 for i in range(h) if (DAY+i)%30==r['mod'])
        if occ:
            events += occ
            cur=(r['eff'] or 0)*(r['seats'] or 1)*occ
            nxt=next_price(r)*(r['seats'] or 1)*occ
            total_cur += cur; total_next += nxt
            k=(r['group_id'],r['plan'])
            by[k][0]+=occ; by[k][1]+=cur; by[k][2]+=nxt; by[k][3]+=r['seats'] or 1
    print('\nH',h,'current_eff',round(total_cur),'recomputed_next',round(total_next),'events',events)
    for k,v in sorted(by.items(), key=lambda kv:-kv[1][2])[:12]: print(' ',k,'events',v[0],'cur',round(v[1]),'next',round(v[2]))

# estimate revenue with retention assumptions per group/plan; use next7 etc
ret={('S2','B'):0.94,('S2','C'):0.98,('S3','B'):0.72,('S3','C'):0.95,('D_S07','B'):0.12,('D_S07','C'):0.9}
for h in [7,14,28,56,70]:
    est=0
    for r in rows:
        occ=sum(1 for i in range(h) if (DAY+i)%30==r['mod'])
        if occ:
            est += next_price(r)*(r['seats'] or 1)*occ*ret.get((r['group_id'],r['plan']),0.8)
    print('H',h,'retention_adj_revenue_est',round(est))
# costs baseline from last week and adjusted configs
# usage last7
usage=q(f"SELECT SUM(usage_units) u FROM daily_usage WHERE day>={DAY-7} AND day<{DAY}")[0]['u']
print('usage_last7',usage,'compute_last7 ledger',q(f"SELECT SUM(amount) a FROM ledger WHERE category='compute' AND day>={DAY-7} AND day<{DAY}")[0]['a'])
print('ledger last7', q(f"SELECT category,SUM(amount) amt FROM ledger WHERE day>={DAY-7} AND day<{DAY} GROUP BY category ORDER BY amt DESC"))
# open enterprise latest
print('enterprise latest senders', q('''WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id) SELECT et.sender, et.day, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.mid JOIN subscriptions s USING(customer_id) JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead' GROUP BY et.sender, et.day ORDER BY et.day DESC, et.sender LIMIT 20'''))
print('enterprise subscribed', q("SELECT COUNT(*) n, SUM(seat_count) seats FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id) WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='subscribed'"))

import json, re, math
from collections import defaultdict
import novamind_api as nm
DAY=nm.vars.current_day

def q(sql):
    try:
        return nm.query(sql).get('rows', [])
    except Exception as e:
        print('QUERY_ERR', e, sql[:500]); return []

print('DAY', DAY)
print('STATUS?')
# active subs grouped billings by mod/group/plan/type
print('\n-- Active subscription aggregate by group/plan/mod --')
rows=q('''SELECT c.customer_type, c.group_id, s.plan, s.billing_day_mod30 AS mod,
          COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats,
          SUM(s.effective_price*s.seat_count) eff_mrr,
          SUM(s.listed_price*s.seat_count) list_mrr,
          AVG(s.promotion) avg_promo, AVG(?-s.start_day) avg_age
          FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
          WHERE s.status='subscribed'
          GROUP BY c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan,s.billing_day_mod30
          ORDER BY c.customer_type,c.group_id,s.plan,s.billing_day_mod30'''.replace('?',str(DAY)))
print('groups', len(rows), 'total_eff_mrr', sum(r['eff_mrr'] or 0 for r in rows), 'total_seats', sum(r['seats'] or 0 for r in rows))
# horizons current configured
for h in [7,14,28,30,35,56,70,84]:
    by=defaultdict(lambda: {'events':0,'amt':0.0,'n':0,'seats':0})
    total=0; events=0; seats=0
    for r in rows:
        occ=sum(1 for i in range(h) if ((DAY+i)%30)==r['mod'])
        if occ:
            amt=(r['eff_mrr'] or 0)*occ
            total+=amt; events+=(r['n'] or 0)*occ; seats+=(r['seats'] or 0)*occ
            k=(r['customer_type'],r['group_id'],r['plan'])
            by[k]['events']+=(r['n'] or 0)*occ
            by[k]['seats']+=(r['seats'] or 0)*occ
            by[k]['amt']+=amt
    print('\nH',h,'configured_eff_billings',round(total,2),'events',events,'seats-events',seats)
    for k,v in sorted(by.items(), key=lambda kv:-kv[1]['amt'])[:15]: print(' ',k,v)

print('\n-- Billing per day next 35 --')
byday=[]
for i in range(35):
    d=DAY+i; mod=d%30
    drows=[r for r in rows if r['mod']==mod]
    total=sum(r['eff_mrr'] or 0 for r in drows)
    n=sum(r['n'] or 0 for r in drows)
    br=defaultdict(lambda:[0,0.0])
    for r in drows:
        br[(r['group_id'],r['plan'])][0]+=r['n'] or 0; br[(r['group_id'],r['plan'])][1]+=r['eff_mrr'] or 0
    print(d, 'mod', mod, 'amt', round(total,2), 'events', n, 'top', sorted([(k,v[0],round(v[1],0)) for k,v in br.items()], key=lambda x:-x[2])[:5])

print('\n-- Active issue aggregate on subscribed customers --')
for r in q('''SELECT i.group_id, COUNT(*) n, AVG(i.days_open) avg_age, MAX(i.days_open) max_age,
              SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open<=1 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) age01,
              SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open BETWEEN 2 AND 3 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) age23,
              SUM(CASE WHEN i.days_open>=4 THEN 1 ELSE 0 END) age4p
              FROM issues i JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=i.customer_id AND s.status='subscribed'
              WHERE i.status='open'
              GROUP BY i.group_id ORDER BY n DESC'''):
    print(r)
print('Resolved last7 active-ish all by group')
for r in q(f'''SELECT i.group_id, COUNT(*) n, AVG(i.days_open) avg_age, MAX(i.days_open) max_age
              FROM issues i WHERE i.status='resolved' AND i.resolved_day>={DAY-7} AND i.resolved_day<{DAY}
              GROUP BY i.group_id ORDER BY n DESC LIMIT 20'''):
    print(r)

print('\n-- Payments last7/30 by group/plan via parsing ledger note --')
# Use substr after last space? note format fixed: customer id after final space.
for h in [7,14,30]:
    print('H',h)
    sql=f'''WITH pays AS (
      SELECT day, amount, CAST(substr(note, instr(note,'customer ')+9) AS INTEGER) AS customer_id
      FROM ledger WHERE category='subscription_payment' AND day>={DAY-h} AND day<{DAY}
    )
    SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, COUNT(*) n, SUM(p.amount) amt, AVG(p.amount) avg_amt
    FROM pays p JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
    LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=p.customer_id AND (s.status='subscribed' OR (s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day>=p.day))
    GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan ORDER BY amt DESC LIMIT 30'''
    for r in q(sql): print(r)

print('\n-- Cancellations/payment ratios by due mod last 14 (small groups) --')
for r in q(f'''WITH canc AS (
  SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, s.end_day AS day, COUNT(*) cancels, SUM(s.effective_price) cancel_mrr
  FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
  WHERE c.customer_type='small' AND s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day>={DAY-14} AND s.end_day<{DAY}
  GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,s.end_day
), pays AS (
  SELECT c.group_id, s.plan, l.day, COUNT(*) pays, SUM(l.amount) amt
  FROM ledger l JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=CAST(substr(l.note, instr(l.note,'customer ')+9) AS INTEGER)
  LEFT JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=c.customer_id AND (s.status='subscribed' OR (s.status='cancelled' AND s.end_day>=l.day))
  WHERE l.category='subscription_payment' AND c.customer_type='small' AND l.day>={DAY-14} AND l.day<{DAY}
  GROUP BY c.group_id,s.plan,l.day
)
SELECT COALESCE(p.day,canc.day) day, COALESCE(p.group_id,canc.group_id) group_id, COALESCE(p.plan,canc.plan) plan,
       COALESCE(p.pays,0) pays, COALESCE(p.amt,0) amt, COALESCE(canc.cancels,0) cancels,
       CASE WHEN COALESCE(p.pays,0)+COALESCE(canc.cancels,0)>0 THEN 1.0*COALESCE(p.pays,0)/(COALESCE(p.pays,0)+COALESCE(canc.cancels,0)) ELSE NULL END AS pay_share
FROM pays p FULL OUTER JOIN canc ON p.day=canc.day AND p.group_id=canc.group_id AND p.plan=canc.plan
ORDER BY day, group_id, plan LIMIT 300'''):
    print(r)

print('\n-- Acquisition/loss leads last14 by small group --')
for r in q(f'''SELECT c.group_id, s.status, s.plan, COUNT(*) n FROM subscriptions s JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='small' AND s.start_day>={DAY-14} AND s.start_day<{DAY}
GROUP BY c.group_id,s.status,s.plan ORDER BY c.group_id,s.status,s.plan'''):
    print(r)

print('\n-- Enterprise latest open message counts --')
for r in q('''WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT c.group_id, et.sender, et.day, COUNT(*) n, SUM(s.seat_count) seats FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.mid JOIN subscriptions s ON s.customer_id=l.customer_id JOIN customers c ON c.customer_id=l.customer_id
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND s.status='lead' GROUP BY c.group_id, et.sender, et.day ORDER BY et.day DESC, seats DESC LIMIT 100'''):
    print(r)

print('\n-- Enterprise latest customer text sample new system --')
for r in q('''WITH latest AS (SELECT customer_id, MAX(message_id) mid FROM enterprise_turns WHERE closed=0 GROUP BY customer_id)
SELECT et.message_id, et.customer_id, c.group_id, et.day, et.sender, et.seat_count, substr(et.message_text,1,500) msg, et.offer_json
FROM latest l JOIN enterprise_turns et ON et.message_id=l.mid JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE c.customer_type='large' AND et.sender='system'
ORDER BY et.day DESC, et.seat_count DESC LIMIT 20'''):
    print(r)

print('\n-- Enterprise agent offers sample day420 --')
for r in q('''SELECT et.customer_id, c.group_id, et.day, et.seat_count, et.offer_json
FROM enterprise_turns et JOIN customers c USING(customer_id)
WHERE et.sender='agent' AND et.day=420
ORDER BY et.seat_count DESC LIMIT 20'''):
    print(r)

print('\n-- Research in-progress rows --')
for r in q('SELECT * FROM research_projects WHERE status="in_progress" ORDER BY start_day'):
    print(r)
